Intent:

Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: TDD public boundary is correct and matches implemented adapter/config/workflow symbols
Tests are non-vacuous and map ISS-1/ISS-2 to P1-P4
no-mistakes cannot rewrite gate authority per the separate-gate test
pytest pass status (168 focused / 776 full) is self_reported per receipts; not re-run in this gate
artifact sha256 pinning not re-verified (hash command DENIED) — self_reported

criteria: All 11 named tests exist verbatim in source and assert against real symbols
build_no_mistakes_command emits exact argv with no default --yes and --skip=push,pr,ci
NoMistakesCfg defaults are safe (policy off, auto_yes False, allow_shipping_steps False)
Separate-gate test asserts outcome_review gate_statuses==[accepted] under required-mode block
confidence.rationale: Every TDD assertion verified non-vacuous against real implementation symbols and behaviors, and the two load-bearing invariants (post-acceptance ordering, separate-gate no-rewrite) are directly asserted. Confidence held below 0.95 because pytest and shasum verification were DENIED in this session, so pass/pinning is self_reported, and tests are GREEN-not-RED.

payload.content: tdd_review ACCEPT. 11 TDD cases map ISS-1/ISS-2 and P1-P4 to real symbols and are non-vacuous at the public boundary (run_no_mistakes_validation, build_no_mistakes_command, NoMistakesCfg, workflow_kwargs_from_payload, run_dual_agent_workflow). Adapter tests prove external/no-vendor, safe defaults (policy off, --skip=push,pr,ci, no default --yes), missing-binary matrix, isolated detached worktree, changed_requires_rerun, and runner-exception cleanup. Workflow tests prove no-mistakes runs AFTER accepted outcome_review (no_mistakes_index>outcome_index) and that required-mode unavailability blocks at a SEPARATE no_mistakes_validation gate while outcome_review gate_statuses==[accepted] (no gate-authority rewrite). critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A pytest run showing any of the 11 named tests failing or absent, or evidence that a no_mistakes event can be written before/over the accepted outcome_review gate.

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Add no-mistakes as an optional external post-outcome supervisor validation service without vendoring code or changing gate authority.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

no-mistakes is integrated as an optional external validator without vendoring and without changing gate authority
Default policy is off; advisory records evidence but only changed_requires_rerun can block in advisory mode
Post-outcome ordering and a separate validation gate preserve the existing outcome_review acceptance
I did not execute pytest in this gate; GREEN status is self-reported from handoff receipts (168 focused / 776 full claimed)

payload.content: Execution gate: the accepted no-mistakes external-validator implementation is fully present and correct in the worktree. supervisor/no_mistakes.py is an external subprocess adapter (no vendoring, runner DI) building 'no-mistakes axi run --intent <intent> --skip=push,pr,ci' with --yes gated to shipping. NoMistakesCfg defaults off (P2). stdio runs validation post-acceptance after outcome_review (P3) and, when blocking, writes a SEPARATE no_mistakes_validation gate without rewriting the outcome_review acceptance (P4); clean-branch runs in an isolated detached worktree with mutation->changed_requires_rerun. File ownership matches the plan exactly (+392/-1 tracked + new no_mistakes.py/test_no_mistakes.py). All 11 planned tests exist and map P1-P4. Verified by source reading; pytest could not be executed in this gate (approval unavailable) so test_status is self-reported.
